C-sUAS Tactical Simulator

Discrete-event Monte Carlo simulation for Counter-sUAS kill web analysis. Build templates, design tactical scenarios, and evaluate system resilience across multi-domain environments.

Welcome to C-sUAS Tactical Simulator

Evaluate system resilience across multi-domain environments.

Quick Start Actions

Load a baseline scenario to explore the tools, or start with a blank slate.

Scenario Overview

No scenario loaded. Click 'Load Built-in Demo' to begin.

Environment Configuration

Ghost track placeholder is disabled.
Clutter field placeholder is disabled.

Live Preview & Execution

Blue object / coverage Red physical object / intent heading Track belief

Select a Lesson

Choose a module to load its quick reference guidance.

Lesson 1

No tutorial guidance provided.

Tutorial Notes

Tutorial cards are content-only in this version. Use them as guided workflow references while building and running scenarios.
Use Run Scenario for actual playback and View Reports for debrief and export surfaces.

Environment Extractor

The extractor remains a standalone utility so terrain extraction, DEM handling, and scenario packaging can evolve without destabilizing the simulator shell. Use the buttons below to launch the external tools, then import the generated scenario JSON back into the simulator.

Recommended Workflow

  • 1. Extract terrainUse the standalone extractor for imagery, DEM, local-relief terrain segmentation, and scenario packaging.
  • 2. Review templatesUse Template Manager for bulk template authoring and reference JSON generation.
  • 3. Import and refineLoad the generated scenario here, then use Scenario Editor for live object and terrain debugging.

Extractor Notes

Current extractor terrain detection uses local relief, not map-wide average height, to emphasize radar-relevant spikes and anomalies.
The standalone utility still handles CORS fallbacks, contour guard rails, and scenario-ready JSON packaging outside the simulator shell.

Scenario Editor

Import, edit, validate, stage, and export complete scenarios while keeping the map visible.

Terrain + Environment

Scenario Metadata

Scenario Review

Blue Asset Groups

Red Threat Groups

Blue Instance Manager

Choose a Blue template, add the instance, select it, then click the map to place it.

Selected Object

Red Instance Manager

Choose a Red template, add the instance, then place or reroute it directly on the draft scenario map.

Selected Object

Scenario Map

The map stays fixed while editor subtabs change. Select an instance and click the map to place it.

No map selection
Blue object / coverage Red object / route Preview state

Implicit C2 Networks

Implicit Power Grids

Template Editor

Deep template editing surface for component structure, power/network posture, subcomponents, and advanced JSON review.

Template Helpers

The helper area now changes with the selected template and can seed common component bundles without dropping into raw JSON.

The latest available single-run output is shown here.

Run Summary

Report Details

    Event Timeline

    Export Preview

    About

    Version context, workflow notes, and companion utility links for the simulator workspace.

    Application
    C-sUAS Tactical Simulator
    Version
    v2.4+
    Primary Workflow
    Edit draft scenario, stage it, run it, then review reports.

    Recommended Workflow

    • 1. Quick StartLoad a built-in demo or blank scenario from Demo & Tutorial.
    • 2. Edit DraftUse Template Editor and Scenario Editor to change the draft scenario in memory.
    • 3. Stage DraftUse Stage Current Scenario to promote the draft into the active scenario used by Run.
    • 4. ExecuteRun single scenarios or Monte Carlo batches from Run Scenario.
    • 5. DebriefUse View Reports for summaries, timelines, and export-ready outputs.

    Companion Utilities

    Standalone helpers stay separate from the simulator shell so they can evolve without destabilizing the main UI.

    Environment Extractor packages map imagery, terrain, and scenario-ready JSON.
    Template Manager supports standalone template review and bulk authoring workflows.
